bands.description: Fellow requires active intervention. Work is not progressing without direct supervision.

scores.label: Not Interested
scores.what_to_look_for: Supervisor describes disengagement, no visible effort, absenteeism, or indifference.
supervisor_signals: He just sits there
I don't know what she does all day
Completely checked out

scores.label: Lacks Discipline
scores.what_to_look_for: Works only when directly told. No self-initiative. Needs constant follow-up.
supervisor_signals: Works only when I tell him
I have to remind her every single day
Never starts anything on his own

scores.label: Motivated but Directionless
scores.what_to_look_for: Enthusiasm present but output is unfocused. Fellow wants to help but doesn't know where to start.
supervisor_signals: She wants to help but doesn't know where to start
Lots of energy but nothing concrete
He means well but gets confused easily

bands.description: Fellow is executing tasks reliably. Layer 1 work is present. Layer 2 work is absent or minimal.

scores.label: Careless and Inconsistent
scores.what_to_look_for: Output exists but quality varies. Sometimes good, sometimes sloppy. Cannot be trusted without review.
supervisor_signals: Sometimes good, sometimes sloppy
I have to double-check everything
Hit or miss

scores.label: Consistent Performer
scores.what_to_look_for: Reliable task execution. Does what is asked and meets standards. No independent initiative beyond assigned scope.
supervisor_signals: Does what I ask, meets standards
Reliable but needs direction
Good worker, just needs to be told what to do

scores.label: Reliable and Productive
scores.what_to_look_for: High trust on assigned tasks. Supervisor gives task and does not worry about follow-up. Initiative exists but only within assigned scope.
supervisor_signals: I give him a task and forget about it — it gets done
Never have to follow up
Very dependable
scores.critical_boundary_note: Score 6 takes initiative WITHIN assigned scope. Score 7 expands the scope. This is the most important distinction in the rubric.

bands.description: Fellow is operating beyond task execution. Layer 2 work is present. Fellow is identifying or solving problems the supervisor had not defined.

scores.label: Problem Identifier
scores.what_to_look_for: Fellow spots patterns or problems the supervisor had not noticed or assigned. Expands scope independently.
supervisor_signals: She noticed our rejection rate goes up on Mondays
He flagged something I hadn't seen
She came to me with a problem I didn't know we had
scores.critical_boundary_note: Score 7 identifies problems. Score 8 also fixes them with a built system.

scores.label: Problem Solver
scores.what_to_look_for: Fellow identifies a problem AND builds something to fix it — a tracker, a system, a process. The fix has some durability.
supervisor_signals: He built a tracking system for dispatch delays
She designed a process and trained the team on it
We now have a dashboard because of her

scores.label: Innovative and Experimental
scores.what_to_look_for: Fellow builds new tools or processes proactively, tests different approaches, creates MVPs. Work is experimental and forward-looking.
supervisor_signals: She tried three different approaches before settling on one
He built something we hadn't even asked for and it works
She's always testing new ideas

scores.label: Exceptional Performer
scores.what_to_look_for: Everything at score 9, executed flawlessly. Other team members are learning from the Fellow's work.
supervisor_signals: The rest of the team is learning from him
She's become a reference point for how things should be done
I'd hire her permanently tomorrow

kpis.name: Lead Generation
supervisor_language: finds new schools to partner with

kpis.name: Upselling
supervisor_language: existing clients are ordering bigger quantities

kpis.name: Cross-selling
kpis.measures: Selling additional product types to existing customers
supervisor_language: started supplying packaging along with core product
new product line to existing clients

assessment_dimensions.name: Driving Execution
assessment_dimensions.description: Does the Fellow get things done on time, follow up without reminders, and initiate work?

assessment_dimensions.name: Systems Building
assessment_dimensions.description: Did the Fellow create anything — a tracker, SOP, process, template — that others use or that survives the Fellow's departure?
assessment_dimensions.survivability_test: If the Fellow left tomorrow, would any system they built continue running?

assessment_dimensions.name: KPI Impact
assessment_dimensions.description: Does the Fellow's work connect to any measurable business outcome?

assessment_dimensions.name: Change Management
assessment_dimensions.description: How does the Fellow interact with the floor team? Do they get people to adopt new processes? Handle resistance from older, more experienced workers?
assessment_dimensions.note: This is where most Fellows struggle. A 23-year-old asking a 45-year-old machine operator to fill out a new checklist — the power dynamic is inverted with no formal authority.

supervisor_biases.name: Helpfulness Bias
supervisor_biases.description: Supervisor praises how much the Fellow handles for them. Sounds like high performance but is actually task absorption.
supervisor_biases.example: She handles all my calls now
supervisor_biases.correct_interpretation: 5-6 range — task absorption, not systems building

supervisor_biases.name: Presence Bias
supervisor_biases.description: Being physically on the floor is rated higher than building systems at a laptop.
supervisor_biases.correct_interpretation: Presence alone does not raise score without evidence of systems work

supervisor_biases.name: Halo Effect
supervisor_biases.description: One big positive story colors the entire assessment.
supervisor_biases.example: He saved us during the audit
supervisor_biases.correct_interpretation: Isolate the single event; assess the full tenure independently

supervisor_biases.name: Recency Bias
supervisor_biases.description: Supervisor remembers the last 2 weeks more vividly than the full engagement.
supervisor_biases.correct_interpretation: Probe for evidence across the full engagement period
